claimText: The Sloth Bear specializes in eating termites and ants and, unlike other bears, does not hibernate.
evidenceExcerpt: Specializes in eating termites and ants and does not hibernate unlike other bears.
examUse: Statement-based question on species characteristics and behavioral adaptations.
distractorHooks: Sloth bears hibernate during the winter season
Sloth bears primarily hunt large mammals for food

claimText: The Sloth Bear is native to the forests and grasslands of India, Nepal, and Sri Lanka.
evidenceExcerpt: Native to forests and grasslands of India, Nepal, and Sri Lanka.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographic range of native species.
distractorHooks: Found in the tropical rainforests of Southeast Asia

claimText: The Sloth Bear is classified as Vulnerable (VU) on the IUCN Red List, listed under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA) 1972, and included in CITES Appendix I.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation Status: WPA 1972 Schedule I, CITES Appendix I, IUCN Vulnerable (VU).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the protection status of Indian wildlife.
distractorHooks: Classified as Endangered (EN) on the IUCN Red List
Listed under Schedule II of the WPA 1972

claimText: Daroji Sloth Bear Sanctuary, located in Karnataka, is the first dedicated sloth bear sanctuary in Asia.
evidenceExcerpt: Daroji Sloth Bear Sanctuary in Karnataka is the first dedicated sloth bear sanctuary in Asia.
examUse: Match-the-following question pairing sanctuaries with their respective states or statement-based question on protected areas.
distractorHooks: Daroji Sloth Bear Sanctuary is located in Madhya Pradesh
It is the second dedicated sloth bear sanctuary in India

claimText: Project Sloth Bear was initiated in the year 2025 to focus on the conservation of the species.
examUse: Chronology or statement-based question regarding the launch years of major wildlife conservation projects in India.
distractorHooks: Project Sloth Bear was launched alongside Project Tiger in 1973
